name: Drupal
description: Enterprise-grade open source CMS with powerful content modeling and extensibility

description.value: Enterprise-grade open source CMS with flexible content architecture, robust permissions, and powerful API capabilities

repository.comment: Mirror of git.drupal.org; PRs not accepted on GitHub

initial-release.comment: Drupal 1.0 released January 15, 2001 by Dries Buytaert

cms-type.comment: Traditional with Twig templates, plus headless via JSON:API (core) and GraphQL (contrib module)

deployment-model.comment: Self-hosted primary; managed options via Acquia, Pantheon, Platform.sh

database-support.comment: MySQL/MariaDB, PostgreSQL, SQLite supported; MySQL/MariaDB most common

api-type.comment: JSON:API in core (recommended for decoupled); REST and GraphQL via contrib modules

multi-tenancy.comment: Drupal Multisite allows multiple sites from single codebase; Domain Access module for virtual multisites

static-site-generation.comment: Via Tome module for static generation, or headless with Gatsby/Next.js

edge-deployment.comment: PHP-based; requires traditional server, though content can be served via CDN

content-types.comment: Core feature: define unlimited custom content types with UI or code

custom-fields.comment: Field API allows adding any number of fields to any entity type

field-types.comment: Extensive field types in core; Paragraphs module for components/repeaters

content-relationships.comment: Entity Reference fields enable one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many relationships

nested-components.comment: Paragraphs module (contrib) provides reusable nested components; Layout Builder in core

localization.comment: Multilingual in core: Language, Content Translation, Interface Translation, Configuration Translation modules

content-validation.comment: Field validation with constraints in core; enhanced via Field Validation contrib module

embedded-interactives.comment: Media embed, oEmbed support in core; custom blocks and paragraphs for interactive content

content-versioning.comment: Content moderation and revisions in core

draft-publish.comment: Workflows module in core with customizable draft/review/publish states

approval-workflows.comment: Content Moderation + Workflows modules in core provide full editorial workflows

content-locking.comment: Content Lock module (contrib) prevents concurrent editing

audit-logging.comment: Database Logging in core; enhanced via Admin Audit Trail contrib module

media-library.comment: Media Library in core since Drupal 8.7

image-transformations.comment: Image styles in core: resize, crop, scale, rotate, desaturate

external-media-storage.comment: S3 File System module for AWS S3 storage

video-support.comment: Remote video (YouTube, Vimeo) in core; local video via contrib modules

svg-support.comment: SVG Image module (contrib) enables SVG uploads

folder-organization.comment: Media folders via File Browser or Media Directories contrib modules

role-based-access.comment: Comprehensive role and permission system in core

granular-permissions.comment: Field-level, content-type-level, and entity-level permissions available

sso-saml.comment: SAML Authentication module (contrib) for SSO integration

team-collaboration.comment: Content moderation workflows, user assignments; Workspaces module in core for staging environments

audience-restrictions.comment: Node access system in core; Group module for closed user groups

typescript-sdk.comment: No official TypeScript SDK; community packages available for JSON:API consumption

content-type-sdk.comment: Configuration API and Recipes allow code-first content modeling; Config Sync for export/import

cli-tools.comment: Drush is the comprehensive CLI for Drupal (Swiss Army knife for developers)

local-development.comment: DDEV, Lando, or Docker-based local environments; official DDEV recommendation

webhooks.comment: Webhooks module (contrib) for event-based HTTP notifications

plugin-system.comment: 50,000+ contrib modules on drupal.org; Plugin API for extensibility

admin-ui-customization.comment: Admin themes, custom admin pages, Gin admin theme popular for modern UX

documentation-quality.comment: Good documentation with user guide, developer docs, API reference; can be overwhelming for beginners

frontend-frameworks.comment: Next.js for Drupal (official module), Druxt for Nuxt, Gatsby source plugin

search-integration.comment: Search API in core; Elasticsearch, Solr, Algolia integrations via contrib

preview-mode.comment: Draft preview in core; decoupled preview via Next.js for Drupal module

ecommerce-integration.comment: Drupal Commerce is a full-featured e-commerce framework

analytics-integration.comment: Google Analytics, Matomo modules available; Statistics in core

ai-features.comment: Drupal AI module with 48+ provider integrations; included in Drupal CMS distribution

cdn-included.comment: Self-hosted; CDN integration via hosting providers like Acquia, Pantheon

response-caching.comment: Internal Page Cache and Dynamic Page Cache in core; BigPipe for lazy-loading

rate-limits.comment: Self-hosted has no limits; managed hosts may impose limits

pricing-model.comment: Core software is free; paid hosting and enterprise support available

commercial-support.comment: Acquia, Pantheon, many agencies offer commercial Drupal support

enterprise-features.comment: Acquia offers enterprise hosting with SSO, SLA, and dedicated support; Workspaces for staging
